metadata.name: eternal_word
metadata.description: Permanent on-chain registry of Bible verses — one account per verse

docs: Eternal Word — permanent, collaborative registry of the Bible on Solana.
There is deliberately no `update` and no `close` instruction anywhere in
this program: immutability comes from no write path existing, not from a
flag someone has to respect.
Handlers are called by full path (`instructions::x::handler`) because the
instruction name and its module name collide otherwise — `seal` is both.

instructions.name: complete_book
docs: Marks a book as fully loaded.

accounts.name: signer
docs: Any signer: completing a book only reads commitment-validated state.

instructions.name: initialize_book_roots
docs: Allocates the roots account of one book, sized from a bytecode constant.

accounts.name: payer
docs: Any wallet: allocating a book's roots account is permissionless. The
account can only be created once (`init`) and its size is fixed by the
bytecode, so an early creator only does the bootstrap's work for free.

instructions.name: initialize_config
docs: Creates the singleton config. The canon's commitment is a bytecode
constant, so there is no parameter and no privileged caller.

accounts.name: payer
docs: Any wallet: creating the config is permissionless. The commitment it
validates against is the `ROOTS_COMMITMENT` constant, so whoever creates
it — including a front-runner — installs the same, real canon.

instructions.name: load_chapter_root
docs: Stores one chapter root, only if it proves into the commitment.

accounts.name: signer
docs: Any signer: loading is permissionless and pays no rent (the account
already exists). Not named `payer` because nothing is paid here.

instructions.name: register_verse
docs: Registers a verse, proving its text against the canonical Merkle root.

accounts.name: config
docs: Read only to gate registration on `sealed`: a verse cannot be adopted
until the canon is declared final. The Merkle check itself does not
need config — the chapter root is validated against the commitment when
it is loaded, and `book_roots` is bound by its own PDA seeds — but
opening registration against a half-loaded canon would let someone
permanently adopt a verse before the text set is complete.

accounts.name: verse_account
docs: Numeric seeds, never book names. `init` is what makes a second
registration of the same verse impossible: the address already exists.

instructions.name: seal
docs: Closes the canon for good.

accounts.name: signer
docs: Any signer: sealing only succeeds when all 66 books are already loaded
and completed against the hardcoded commitment, so there is nothing to
gate — whoever triggers it just finalizes the one correct canon.

errors.name: ConfigSealed
errors.msg: Config is sealed; the canonical text can no longer be changed

errors.name: BookOutOfRange
errors.msg: Book index is outside the canon (expected 1 to 66)

errors.name: ChapterOutOfRange
errors.msg: Chapter does not exist in this book

errors.name: ProofTooLong
errors.msg: Merkle proof is longer than the commitment tree allows

errors.name: RootNotCommitted
errors.msg: Chapter root does not belong to the committed canonical text

errors.name: BookIncomplete
errors.msg: Book roots are incomplete; every chapter must be loaded first

errors.name: CanonIncomplete
errors.msg: Not every book is complete; all 66 must be loaded before sealing

errors.name: ChapterRootMissing
errors.msg: Chapter root has not been loaded yet

errors.name: TextTooLong
errors.msg: Verse text is longer than any verse in the canonical text

errors.name: VerseNotCanonical
errors.msg: Verse does not belong to the canonical text at this address

errors.name: CanonNotSealed
errors.msg: The canon is not sealed yet; registration is not open

types.name: BookRoots
docs: Chapter roots of a single book, at a PDA seeded by the book index.
Sharded per book because all 1,189 roots in one account would be 38,048
bytes — over the 10,240-byte limit the runtime allows an account to grow
per instruction. The largest book (Psalms, 150 chapters) needs 4,800 bytes
and fits in a single creation, so no account here ever needs realloc.

fields.name: loaded
docs: How many roots are filled. Complete when it equals the book's chapters.

fields.name: completed
docs: Set once by `complete_book`. Without it, calling `complete_book` twice
for the same book would increment `Config::books_complete` twice, and a
single loaded book completed 66 times would let `seal` close an
incomplete canon — permanently. This flag makes completion idempotent.

fields.name: loaded_mask
docs: Bitmap of loaded chapters, so a rewrite is never counted twice.
One bit per chapter, `(chapter - 1)` indexed.

fields.name: roots
docs: Chapter roots, `(chapter - 1)` indexed.

types.name: Config
docs: Global configuration. One per program, at a PDA of fixed seeds.
Holds no commitment and no authority: the commitment is the
`ROOTS_COMMITMENT` constant in the bytecode, and every bootstrap
instruction is permissionless. This account only tracks progress —
how many books are loaded and whether the canon is sealed. Its fixed seeds
still matter: `register_verse` reads `sealed` from it, so accepting an
arbitrary account here would let someone fake a sealed canon.

fields.name: translation
docs: Translation identifier of the frozen snapshot.

fields.name: books_complete
docs: Books whose roots are fully loaded. Sealing requires all 66.

fields.name: sealed
docs: Once true, no instruction writes to config or roots ever again.

types.name: VerseAccount
docs: A registered verse. One account per verse, created once and never written
again — the existence of the account *is* the registration.
There is no `update` and no `close` instruction for this account anywhere
in the program. That is the whole guarantee: permanence comes from the
absence of a write path, not from a flag.

fields.name: adopter
docs: Wallet that paid for and registered this verse. Never named `owner`:
on Solana that word already means the program that owns the account
(docs/conventions/UBIQUITOUS_LANGUAGE.md).

fields.name: created_at
docs: Unix timestamp of the registering slot.

fields.name: text
docs: The canonical text itself. On-chain by design — the premise of the
product is that the text lives on the chain, not a pointer to it.

types.name: VerseRegistered
docs: Emitted on every successful registration so the indexer can record it in
real time without re-reading the account. Carries only what the program
knows at registration; the indexer enriches it with the transaction
signature and the confirmation slot to build the domain event
(docs/conventions/UBIQUITOUS_LANGUAGE.md → VerseRegistered).

constants.name: CONFIG_SEED
docs: PDA seeds.
Seeds are numeric on purpose: book names never appear in an address
(docs/conventions/UBIQUITOUS_LANGUAGE.md bans string seeds).
